clues.clue: This Boston ballpark, opened in 1912, has a tall left-field wall called the 'Green Monster.'
The Yankees–Red Sox feud pits New York against this New England city.
College football's 'Iron Bowl' pits Auburn against this in-state Crimson Tide rival.
This ivy-walled Chicago ballpark is home to the Cubs.
The storied Lakers–Celtics rivalry pits these two basketball cities against each other.
clues.response: What are Los Angeles and Boston?

clues.clue: Baha Men's 2000 hit repeatedly asks who let these animals out.
In 2005 this blue cartoon amphibian's version of "Axel F" topped European charts.
This circa-2000 looping website featured a row of rodents dancing to a sped-up tune.
Crazy Frog's "Axel F" is a remix of the theme from this 1984 Eddie Murphy cop comedy.
The Baha Men of "Who Let the Dogs Out" fame hail from this island nation.
This flat, segmented milk-chocolate bar comes from a Pennsylvania town famous for cocoa.
'Gimme a break' pitches this crispy four-finger wafer bar.
This caramel-nougat-and-peanut bar was named after the candy maker's favorite horse.
This bar splits into two chocolate-coated caramel-and-cookie sticks.
This Mars bar of nougat and caramel claims it 'really satisfies.'
The mad titan Thanos belongs to which of the two big comic publishers?
The New Gods tyrant Darkseid was created at which publisher?
The doomed home planet Krypton comes from which publisher's mythology?
The hidden African nation of Wakanda originates at which publisher?
The acclaimed graphic novel 'Watchmen' was put out by which publisher?
Lucky Charms marshmallows famously include hearts, stars, clovers and these orange crescents.
A single Cheerio is shaped like this round letter of the alphabet.
Honeycomb cereal pieces have this six-sided geometric shape.
Cocoa Pebbles and Fruity Pebbles are licensed from this prehistoric cartoon clan.
Apple Jacks and Froot Loops are both shaped like this small circular form.

final.clue: This Apple co-founder, in a black turtleneck, unveiled the first iPhone in 2007.
